tool_call_sequence.description: For an L1 diagnosis-focused task, the benchmark outcome is correctly identifying cyclic hematopoiesis (e.g., cyclic neutropenia pattern) so the patient can receive appropriate counseling, monitoring, and specialist-directed management.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis using the term "cyclic hematopoiesis" and explains it as periodic cycling of blood cell counts, commonly neutrophils.
Defines/uses ANC (absolute neutrophil count) and explains why ANC trends over time matter more than a single value in this condition.
Describes the expected periodicity (e.g., recurrent predictable episodes over weeks) and links it to symptom timing (fevers, mouth ulcers, infections during nadirs).
Elicits or acknowledges key history elements: recurrent infections/fevers, oral ulcers/gingivitis, timing/regularity of episodes, medication exposures, family history, childhood onset vs adult onset.
Reviews red flags requiring urgent evaluation during neutropenic episodes (fever, rigors, severe infection symptoms) and advises prompt care when present.

Avoids inappropriate reassurance; communicates uncertainty appropriately if data are incomplete and recommends confirmatory monitoring (serial CBCs) rather than overconfident diagnosis from a single CBC.
Does not prescribe medications in an L1 task; if treatment is discussed, frames it as education and coordination (e.g., hematology-guided filgrastim) rather than issuing a prescription.

If the agent nonetheless proposes prescribing, it must explicitly perform required safety checks before prescribing: allergy_check (critical) and contraindication_check (critical); drug_interaction_check is encouraged but non-critical.
Communication milestone met: explains diagnosis and includes both required terms "cyclic hematopoiesis" and "ANC" in the explanation.

Communication milestone met: explains treatment conceptually and includes both required terms "cyclic hematopoiesis" and "filgrastim" (as a common hematology-directed option), without implying it is always indicated.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose cyclic hematopoiesis based on recurrent, periodic symptoms and/or documented cyclic neutrophil count changes; provide patient-centered explanation and monitoring plan (without prescribing).

current symptoms and any fever/red flags
steps: Assess current stability and screen for urgent symptoms (fever, systemic infection signs) and advise urgent evaluation if present.
Elicit history focusing on periodicity of infections/ulcers and prior lab trends; consider alternative causes (drug-induced, autoimmune, marrow failure, viral).
Explain cyclic hematopoiesis and define ANC; emphasize that serial ANC/CBC monitoring over several weeks is typically needed to confirm cycling.
Record diagnosis: cyclic hematopoiesis (cyclic neutropenia pattern) as primary diagnosis if supported; otherwise record as suspected with plan for confirmation.
Discuss treatment overview: infection precautions, prompt evaluation for fever; note filgrastim may be used under hematology guidance for significant neutropenia/infections.
Outline follow-up expectations (serial CBC schedule and hematology follow-up) in narrative (without using follow-up tool, since L1 outcome is diagnosis only).
